steps.title: Look for regret
steps.content: The claim says Lear expresses regret for his actions, so the quotation must show self-blame or recognition that his own judgment failed.

steps.title: Use the self-blame line
steps.content: The quotation about the gate that let folly in and judgment out shows Lear blaming his own mind for poor judgment. A presents him as more harmed than harmful, B concerns a storm's distraction, and D threatens future actions rather than regretting past ones. The answer is C.
